tasks.title: Delete legacy react.py and react_legacy registry entry

RETRIEVAL_PIPELINE.md: ReAct row shows only native implementation
No broken imports anywhere (grep for 'from harness.workflows.react import' returns nothing)
pytest tests/ passes

tasks.title: Remove dead cache: field from all YAML configs
acceptance_criteria: cache: field removed from all 20 YAML configs under harness/configs/
Dead warning block in run_eval.py (lines 71-73) removed
pytest tests/ passes

tasks.title: Fix broken import in run_b1_sanity.py
acceptance_criteria: run_b1_sanity.py no longer imports from harness.agents.react_agent
Agent constructed via get_workflow('react', index=index, llm=get_llm('agent'))
_run_questions updated to extract trajectory from WorkflowRunner result dict (result['trajectory'])
harness/agents/__init__.py deleted if react_agent.py truly does not exist
python -m ablations.B_process_rewards.run_b1_sanity --dry-run succeeds without ImportError
NOTE: full re-run to confirm trajectories is deferred to HITL-005 (after react fix)

tasks.title: Diagnose react workflow failure with Opus swap
acceptance_criteria: models.yaml agent role temporarily changed to claude_opus (or claude_sonnet-4-6)
workflow_react.yaml run on 8 benchmark questions (2 per type: T1-001/T1-002, T2-001/T2-002, T3-001/T3-002, T4-001/T4-002)
Generated answers inspected: do they contain 'Final Answer:' with substantive content?
Per-sample correctness scores recorded (manual or judge)
Findings documented in .claude/work/2026-05-24_12_hitl-pipeline-gaps/react_diagnosis.md
Conclusion: (a) Opus fixes it → problem is model capacity; (b) Opus also fails → problem is prompt; (c) partial improvement → both

tasks.title: Fix react workflow based on diagnosis
acceptance_criteria: If diagnosis shows model capacity issue: models.yaml agent role set to claude_opus permanently (or claude_sonnet as cost compromise); document in DECISIONS.md
If diagnosis shows prompt issue: _SYSTEM_PROMPT and _format_react_prompt in react_native.py revised; Haiku re-tested
Re-run 8-question sample: all 8 produce substantive 'Final Answer:' responses (no truncation/refusal)
DECISIONS.md updated with react model/prompt decision and reasoning

tasks.title: Re-run b1_sanity and confirm trajectories populated
acceptance_criteria: python -m ablations.B_process_rewards.run_b1_sanity runs without error
b1_trajectories.jsonl has 5 rows with trajectory len > 0 for all entries
All 5 entries have non-empty answer field
Trajectories include at least one ema_search tool call per question

tasks.title: Build harness/label_session.py — interactive labeling CLI
acceptance_criteria: python -m harness.label_session --workflow react --config harness/configs/workflow_react.yaml --n 20 --sample stratified runs end-to-end
--sample stratified draws 5 questions from each of T1/T2/T3/T4 (with replacement if type has fewer than 5)
--sample uniform is also supported (random N from full benchmark)
Checkpoint written to ~/Nextcloud/Datasets/ema_nlp/label_sessions/{session_id}.jsonl after each question
Re-running with same --session-id (or auto-detected from checkpoint) skips already-rated questions
Each question: displays answer + cited qa_ids, calls prompt_for_rating() from harness/rating.py
Skipped ratings (user presses Enter) are recorded as None, not as 0
End-of-session summary prints: total rated, skipped, avg rating, question type breakdown
Checkpoint file format: JSONL rows with {bench_id, type, question, answer_text, cited_qa_ids, rating, run_id, workflow, timestamp}
NOTE: --workflow can be any key in WORKFLOW_REGISTRY, not just react

tasks.title: Wire get_fewshot_context() as shared adapter in app.py and run_eval.py
acceptance_criteria: app.py: few_shot_block built via get_fewshot_context(query_vec, cache, k=3, min_rating=4) instead of direct CacheEntry.answer_summary lookup
app.py: query_vec obtained from embed model (same embedding used for cache lookup) before calling get_fewshot_context
app.py: injection still fails closed (no injection if get_fewshot_context returns None)
run_eval.py: reads cache_inject: bool from config (default False)
run_eval.py: when cache_inject=True, calls get_fewshot_context() before WorkflowRunner.invoke and passes result as few_shot_context
run_eval.py: when cache_inject=False (default), few_shot_context is empty string (current behavior preserved)
cache_inject: false added to all eval YAML configs
No cache_inject field added to interactive app.py path (app.py always injects when cache has rated entries)
Unit test: get_fewshot_context called with mock cache having 0, 2, and 3+ rated entries — verifies None/None/str returns

notes.HITL-001_and_002_parallel: HITL-001 and HITL-002 are independent and can be done in a single session
notes.HITL-003_partial: HITL-003 makes the script runnable; HITL-005 confirms trajectories are good. Separated because HITL-004b (react fix) is required for meaningful trajectories.
notes.HITL-004a_sample_size: Diagnostic runs on 8 questions (2 per type) to limit Opus API cost. bench_ids: T1-001, T1-002, T2-001, T2-002, T3-001, T3-002, T4-001, T4-002
notes.HITL-006_no_react_dep: HITL-006 depends on HITL-004b for test quality, but the label_session --workflow flag supports CRAG or any other workflow. If react diagnosis takes time, P0.4 can start with --workflow crag.
notes.HITL-007_replaces_app_logic: P1.1 replaces the current app.py CacheEntry.answer_summary similarity-based injection with the rating-filtered get_fewshot_context() path. This is stricter (min 3 rated entries with rating>=4) so injection frequency drops until the labeling loop produces enough rated examples.
